title: cannot use loop device in podman container
body:
### Issue Description

My usecase is that I want to use a loop device inside a podman container to mount an iso as follows:
podman run --rm -it  -v $PWD:$PWD -w $PWD --privileged=true --cap-add SYS_ADMIN -v /dev:/dev fedora bash
> mkdir /mnt/test
>mount -loop my-test.iso /mnt/test
>stderr output mount: /mnt/test: failed to setup loop device for my-test.iso

Same with docker works flawless Docker version 20.10.12, build 20.10.12-0ubuntu4

Testimage I used was from here https://archive.org/details/tiny-iso-test

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run --rm -it  -v $PWD:$PWD -w $PWD --privileged=true --cap-add SYS_ADMIN -v /dev:/dev fedora bash
2. mkdir /mnt/test
3. mount -loop my-test.iso /mnt/test
4. mount: /mnt/test: failed to setup loop device for my-test.iso

### Describe the results you received

mount of iso failed with error message output mount: /mnt/test: failed to setup loop device for my-test.iso

### Describe the results you expected

Mount of iso works

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

The same happens on a RHEL8.7 x86_64 system with podman version 4.2.0

Please provide some guidance how to solve this problem and provide an evaluation if this is a bug in podman or if it works as designed.

I also can try other ideas or suggestions mounting a loop device.

Thanks

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Did you try this in rootful mode, Mounting a loop device in rootless mode is not allowed by the kernel.  It would only work for Docker if the docker daemon is running as root.
Reopen if this fails in rootful mode.
Thank you for the speedy reply and for the hint, I will try it. Maybe another way is to allow users to mount loop devices. I will investigate.
